title: Failure is terrifying — fitting in vs frequency
html:
TL;DR

  • Three-line meditation: "Failure is terrifying and humiliating. It means not fitting in. Fitting in isn't as nice."
  • Frames not-fitting-in as a frequency-mismatch problem, not a worth problem. "They have searched with people to whom they are on the same frequency with. Is it Silicon Valley, is it in jail. Different kinds of people."
  • Closes with the haunting line: "Always will be a place where you fit in, always never likely feels right. Like a massive bass behind you thumping."
  • Specimen of founder loneliness 2022 2026 — the synthesis page already exists; this is one of its earliest entries.
  • "Tetris photo does have credibility" — orphan opening line, may reference a meme/screenshot that's not in the corpus.

Key quotes

"Failure is terrifying and humiliating. It means not fitting in."

"Fitting in isn't as nice"

"Always will be a place where you fit in, always never likely feels right. Like a massive bass behind you thumping"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Direct feeder into founder loneliness 2022 2026 synthesis (already exists). Add this summary to its arc.
  • The Silicon Valley vs jail dichotomy is the same shape as 2024 10 30 thoughts on future — "the route that is expected of you" vs "sandboard in our own lane".
  • The bass-behind-you image is reusable for any future EXPLORE on identity-frequency.

Open questions

  • The "Tetris photo" — what was it?
  • Was something specific failing in his life Jan 2022 that triggered this? (Sophomore winter term, no relationship listed.)

Wikilinks

founder loneliness 2022 2026 · corvallis or · osu · fear of not doing
